We propose a top-performing multi-modal 3D semantic segmentation method termed MSeg3D.
Unlike separately extracting modal features in existing methods, we jointly optimize intra-modal feature extraction and inter-modal feature fusion.
The multi-modal segmentation works discard many useful and critical point cloud augmentation transformations with sacrificed perception performance.

We propose a top-performing multi-modal 3D semantic segmentation method termed MSeg3D, inherently motivated by addressing the aforementioned three technical difficulties.
We jointly optimize intra-modal feature extraction and inter-modal feature fusion to drive maximum correlation and complementarity between heterogeneous modalities.
To overcome the disregarded multi-modal fusion outside FOV intersection, we propose a cross-modal feature.
The cross-modal feature completion phase collaborates with the geometry-based feature fusion phase to overcome the disregarded multi-modal fusion outside FOVintersection.
The former completes the missing camera intersection features using predicted pseudo-camera features.
The SF-Phase collaborates with the GF-Phase for feature fusion.
The SF-Phase leverages multi-head attention to model the semantic relation between points and interested categories for attentive fusion of semantic embeddings.
The challenging multi-modal data augmentation is decomposed as asymmetric transformations in the LiDAR, camera worlds, and local cameras for flexible permutation to enrich training samples.
The segmentation model MSeg3D includes joint intra-modal feature extraction and inter-modal feature fusion for achieving state-of-the-art 3D segmentation performance.
MSeg3D achieves state-of-the-art performance on competitive datasets such as nuScenes, Waymo, and SemanticKITTI for autonomous driving.

MSeg3D is a novel multi-modal 3D semantic segmentation method for autonomous driving, based on LiDAR and multi-camera sensors.
MSeg3D's cross-modal feature completion and semantic-based feature fusion solve the overlooked problem, enabling multi-modal fusion beyond the sensor FOV intersection.
MSeg3D's asymmetric multi-modal data augmentation enables effective training of the multi-modal segmentation model with reliable performance.
The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance on nuScenes, Waymo, and other platforms.
